texto: When the world careens out of control, we can rest in the fact that God spun this world with a simple word. Matter from emptiness. Beauty from void. Community from chaos.

texto: It’s never easy letting go. But if we don’t learn the art of relinquishment, we’ll never move forward to embrace the new relationships God has for us.

texto: Why must we cling to those who walk away instead of granting freedom? We must give the same liberty God gives to prodigals-an ability to let them go-or we'll be perennially bound to others for our happiness and effective service.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Mary E. DeMuth que provoca temas ligados a felicidade. Ao questionar “Why must we cling to those who walk away instead of granting freedom? We must give the same liber…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre felicidade, freedom, god, com foco em god e happiness.

texto: We don’t like death. We’d rather produce seeds another way. But death to ourselves, our agendas, our expectations, our hopes is necessary to find deep joy that comes when we fully relinquish ourselves to the gospel.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Mary E. DeMuth: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a coragem. O trecho “We don’t like death. We’d rather produce seeds another way. But death to ourselves, our agendas, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que coragem, agenda, death pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.

texto: We enslave in the manner we talk to ourselves. But the truth is, God already set us free. He secured our release. To constantly hurt ourselves, resting in our inadequacy, is to call Him a LIAR.
